title: rosa canina var. hailstonii
intro: Rosa canina var. hailstonii, also known as the dwarf or small-fruited dog rose, is a variety of the Rosa canina species native to Europe and Western Asia. This deciduous shrub has medicinal properties, particularly in its hips, which are rich in vitamins A and C, as well as flavonoids and anthocyanins. The health benefits of Rosa canina var. hailstonii include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The hips of this plant are often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and jams, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
